finding: OIDC signature verification must bind key selection to fresh JWKS material. A previously valid key set must be revalidated when its effective freshness lifetime expires, and key selection must fail closed unless exactly one RS256 signing key matches the token kid.
design_implication: Insert evaluateJwksFreshness and selectFreshVerificationKey between bounded JWKS retrieval and cryptographic signature verification. Apply the stricter of origin max-age and a local maximum TTL; reject no-store, no-cache, stale, duplicate-kid, and non-signing-key states.

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical pre-commit files

uncertainty: HTTP freshness and JWK metadata validation do not verify TLS transport, JWT signature bytes, issuer control, or authorization claim truth.
falsification_route: Demonstrate that the integrated verifier accepts an expired JWKS, ignores response Age, accepts duplicate kid values, or selects a key not marked for RS256 signatures; any such result falsifies the policy implementation.
privacy_boundary: No tokens, credentials, private claims, chat material, or health information are stored.
